Off Calendar Dec-23-2008 Continued To Feb-11-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman for and with Wendy Brummer.

The Court stated that the accounting needed to be amended as it included Sheila's accounting which had already been approved. Mr. Freedman stated that the approved account was not accurate as to the date of death assets and that it would be more difficult to do it as a seperate schedule rather than start at the beginning. In addition, Mr. Freedman explained the extent of services that Ms. Brummer had provided. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Aquino.

Off Calendar May-12-2008 Continued To Jun-09-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman for Wendy Brummer, Trustee.

Mr. Freedman stated that he was not able to complete a full accounting, that he was working on the final distribution to Ms. Schonfeld who resides in France and requested a 30 day continuance. The Court continued the matter to June 9, 2008 for proof that each individual received the distribution and proof of the money being deposited into a blocked account for the remaining minor.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

Successor Trustee Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets And Transferring Assets Into Trust
Off Calendar Jan-17-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman for Wendy Brummer (Petitioner).

Mr. Freedman reported that the title of the MFS account and the Franklin Templeton account was held in the 1995 Trust, but not transferred into the 2002 Trust. In addition, he expressed concern about the error in the grant deed regarding the real property that was sold by Ms. Sexton. In regards to the acconting, Mr. Freedman stated that he could not get the receipts, and that he did not know the amounts of the two trust assets listed above. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Successor Trustee Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets And Transferring Assets Into Trust
Off Calendar Dec-05-2007 Continued To Jan-15-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman for Wendy Brummer.

The Court asked for a copy of the family trust. Mr. Freedman stated that a copy of the 2002 trust was attached to the petition and provided a copy of the 1995 trust. The Court stated that notice was improper. Mr. Freedman reported that there was a waiver of notice from Roberta and that the other beneficiaries received notice by mail. A proposed order was submitted. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Rich Lee.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar May-23-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Michael Freedman appearing for and with trustee Wendy Brummer. The trustee explained how she arrived in identificating the organization Native Americian Foundation. Issue on how to distribute to minor beneficiaries addressed. Counsel wants to have distributions into blocked accounts to minors's parents. Court questioned the validity of the handwritten amendments. Counsel addressed the issue under Probate Code Section 15401. Court is taking the matter under submission. Counsel's request to prepare a separate order under Heggstad to transfer assets is granted.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: M. Loos.
